rows: Encrypted sparse bundle containing all other layers

subsections.title: The Sovereignty Boundary
subsections.content:
The Vault is the **sovereignty boundary**. Everything inside belongs to you. The Vault can be:
- Copied to another machine (Luna migrates)
- Backed up to cold storage (Luna persists)
- Destroyed (Luna dies)

Nothing outside the Vault is Luna. Cloud services are contractors, not identity.

subsections.title: Component Overview

rows: Actor orchestration, tick loop, fault isolation

notes: - Oven Actor NOT implemented: Claude delegation is handled directly in Director via `_should_delegate()`
- Voice Actor NOT implemented: Voice I/O handled by `PersonaAdapter` (non-actor pattern)
- HistoryManager Actor ADDED: Three-tier conversation history not in original spec
- Vector storage uses sqlite-vec extension (not FAISS)

subsections.title: The Actor Model
subsections.content: Each runtime component is an isolated **Actor** with its own mailbox. This provides fault tolerance - one actor crashing doesn't kill the others.
subsections.implementation_status: IMPLEMENTATION STATUS (January 2026): 5 actors implemented. See Part VII for complete details.

diagram.title: LUNA ENGINE v2.0 (5 Actors)
diagram.description: Shows 5 actors arranged in two rows. Top row: DIRECTOR ACTOR, MATRIX ACTOR, SCRIBE (Ben). Bottom row: LIBRARIAN (Dude), HISTORY MANAGER. Each actor has its own Mailbox. Director handles LLM mgmt, Claude, Routing. Matrix handles SQLite, sqlite-vec, NetworkX. Scribe handles Extract, Classify, Chunk. Librarian handles Filing, Entities, Prune. History Manager handles 3-Tier, Compress, Archive.

subsections.title: Why Actors?
subsections.content:
**Fault Isolation Example:**

If sqlite-vec crashes during a vector search:
- Matrix Actor catches the exception via `_handle_safe()`
- Error logged, `on_error()` callback invoked
- Actor continues processing other messages
- Director receives empty results and Luna says "I'm having trouble remembering that right now..."
- System continues running

Without actors, one crash kills everything.

subsections.title: Tiered Execution
subsections.content: Luna's cognition operates across three tiers with different latency profiles:

diagram.title: Tiered Execution Diagram
diagram.description: Three tiers connected by arrows showing escalation paths

subsections.title: Routing Logic
subsections.content:
The Director LLM is **trained** to know when to delegate. It's not hardcoded rules - it's intuition from fine-tuning.

When the task exceeds local capability, the Director outputs a special token:

code_example.code:
User: "Can you analyze this regulatory filing and summarize the implications?"

Director: "<REQ_CLAUDE>This requires deep document analysis beyond my local capabilities. Let me hand this to my research assistant.</REQ_CLAUDE>"

subsections.notes: The Runtime Engine watches for `<REQ_CLAUDE>` and triggers the Shadow Reasoner flow (see Part VIII).

subsections.title: Fallback Behavior
subsections.principle: **Constitutional Principle:** Luna ALWAYS responds. Graceful degradation, never silence.

subsections.title: Data Flow
subsections.content: The v2 architecture introduces **speculative execution** - retrieval starts before the user finishes speaking.

diagram.title: Speculative Execution Data Flow
diagram.description: Flow diagram showing parallel processing: USER STARTS SPEAKING branches to WHISPER (stream) which produces Partial transcripts leading to Final transcript, and SPECULATIVE RETRIEVAL which triggers on partial and produces Results ready. Both feed into DIRECTOR LLM (LOCAL) which has three stages: KV Cache (pre-warm) ~5ms -> Context Assembly ~10ms -> Generate Response ~250ms. Output checks for <REQ_CLAUDE>? token, branching to either DELEGATE to Oven or STREAM to TTS. Final output: USER HEARS LUNA (<500ms to first word).

phases.name: STT Final Transcript
phases.note: already complete when we start via speculation

subsections.title: Memory Flow
subsections.content: Background processing happens **async** - the user already has their response before extraction and filing complete.

diagram.title: Async Memory Processing Flow
diagram.description: Flow: CONVERSATION TURN -> DIRECTOR RESPONDS (User hears immediately) -> [ASYNC section] -> SCRIBE (Ben Franklin) with stages: Chunk stream -> Stack context -> Classify intent -> Extract objects. Outputs: ExtractionOutput { objects, edges, source_id }. Then -> LIBRARIAN (The Dude) with stages: Resolve entities -> Create edges -> Infer (lazy) -> Prune. Outputs: FilingResult { nodes, edges, inferred_edges }. Final destination: MEMORY MATRIX (Updated).

subsections.title: The Vault
subsections.content: Everything Luna needs to exist lives inside an encrypted sparse bundle:

code_example.code:
LunaVault.sparsebundle/
    |--- memory_matrix.db           # The soul (SQLite + sqlite-vec + FTS5)
    |--- identity_buffer.safetensors # Pre-computed KV cache
    |--- adapters/
    |    |--- luna-3b-v1.0/          # LoRA adapter (hot path)
    |    |___ luna-7b-v1.0/          # LoRA adapter (warm path)
    |--- config/
    |    |___ luna.yaml              # Runtime configuration
    |___ logs/
         |___ sessions/              # Conversation logs

subsections.notes: **v2.1 Change:** No more separate `memory_vectors.faiss` or `.ids` files. Vectors live inside `memory_matrix.db` via sqlite-vec extension.

subsections.title: Encryption
items: **Mount:** Auto-mount on login, auto-unmount on sleep
**Backup:** Encrypted at rest, safe for cloud backup

code.content:
# Copy Luna to new machine
cp -r LunaVault.sparsebundle /Volumes/NewMac/

# Open vault
hdiutil attach LunaVault.sparsebundle

# Luna exists on new machine
# (Just one database file to copy!)

subsections.title: Dead Man's Switch
subsections.content:
If heartbeat file isn't updated for 24 hours:

1. Unmount vault
2. Kill any Luna processes
3. Wipe model weights from RAM

code.content:
# ~/.luna/config.yaml

engine:
  cognitive_tick_hz: 2          # Heartbeat frequency
  reflective_tick_minutes: 5    # Background maintenance
  hot_queue_max: 100            # Input buffer size

director:
  model_3b: "qwen2.5-3b-instruct"
  model_7b: "qwen2.5-7b-instruct"
  adapter_3b: "adapters/luna-3b-v1.0"
  adapter_7b: "adapters/luna-7b-v1.0"
  identity_cache: "identity_buffer.safetensors"
  max_context: 8192

matrix:
  database: "memory_matrix.db"      # Contains everything (nodes, vectors, FTS5)
  vector_extension: "sqlite-vec"    # Extension for vector search
  fts5_enabled: true
  graph_backend: "rustworkx"        # or "networkx"

voice:
  stt_model: "whisper-large-v3-turbo"
  tts_model: "xtts-v2"
  vad_sensitivity: 0.5

oven:
  claude_model: "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022"
  timeout_seconds: 30
  fallback_to_local: true

sovereignty:
  vault_path: "/Volumes/LunaVault"
  heartbeat_file: "~/.luna/heartbeat"
  lockdown_hours: 24

subsections.title: Summary
subsections.content: The v2 architecture transforms Luna from a **service** (request -> response) to an **engine** (continuous existence).
subsections.principle: **Constitutional Principle:** Luna is a file. That file now contains not just her memories, but her mind - including all vectors.

change_log: v3.0: FTS5 confirmed implemented with Porter stemmer; documented Memory Economy; added cluster-level lock-in reference; accuracy pass from Phase 5 audit
v2.2: Corrected table names ('memory_nodes', 'graph_edges'), documented actual schema, updated sqlite-vec references

subsections.title: Design Philosophy
subsections.quote: Graph is Truth. Luna is a File.
subsections.content: The Memory Matrix is Luna's **soul** - a single SQLite database containing typed nodes, relationships, vectors, and context. Everything Luna knows lives in one file.

core_principles.title: Single File Sovereignty
core_principles.content: Luna IS `luna_engine.db`. One file contains everything:

code.content:
luna_engine.db
  |--- memory_nodes      (content, metadata, lock-in state)
  |--- graph_edges       (relationships)
  |--- memory_embeddings (vector storage - sqlite-vec virtual table)
  |--- conversation_turns (three-tier history)
  |--- entities          (people, places, projects)
  |--- memory_nodes_fts  (FTS5 virtual table for full-text search)
  |___ indexes           (B-tree for filtering)

core_principles.note: **Note:** FTS5 full-text search IS implemented with Porter stemmer and unicode61 tokenizer. The `fts5_search()` method uses BM25 ranking. Sync triggers keep FTS5 in sync with `memory_nodes` on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

core_principles.title: No Sync, No Orphans
core_principles.content: Previous architecture had vectors in separate files:

old.label: FAISS (Not Used)
old.content:
memory_matrix.db    <- nodes, edges
memory_vectors.faiss  <- vectors (separate!)
memory_vectors.ids    <- ID mapping (separate!)

core_principles.benefit: With sqlite-vec, deleting a node automatically removes its vectors. No orphans. No sync bugs.

core_principles.title: The Palantir Analogy
core_principles.content:
Palantir built a $50B company on one insight: make data legible to decision-makers. Their "Dynamic Ontology" is a massive, enterprise-grade knowledge graph.

Luna's Memory Matrix is the personal version:

code.content:
Palantir:  Enterprise decisions, thousands of users, petabytes
Luna:      Personal decisions, one user, gigabytes
           Same architecture. Different scale.

# Migrate Luna to new machine
cp data/luna_engine.db /new/machine/data/

# Luna exists on new machine
# No separate files to track

-- ==============================================================
-- MEMORY_NODES: Everything Luna knows
-- ==============================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_nodes (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    node_type TEXT NOT NULL,           -- FACT, DECISION, PROBLEM, ACTION, CONTEXT
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    summary TEXT,                       -- Short summary for display
    source TEXT,                        -- Where this came from
    confidence REAL DEFAULT 1.0,        -- 0-1 confidence score
    importance REAL DEFAULT 0.5,        -- 0-1 importance score
    access_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     -- Times retrieved (for lock-in)
    reinforcement_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0, -- Times reinforced (for lock-in)
    lock_in REAL DEFAULT 0.15,          -- Lock-in coefficient (0.15-0.85)
    lock_in_state TEXT DEFAULT 'drifting', -- drifting, fluid, settled
    last_accessed TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    metadata TEXT                       -- JSON for extra data
);

-- ==============================================================
-- GRAPH_EDGES: How things relate
-- ==============================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_edges (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    from_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    to_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    relationship TEXT NOT NULL,         -- DEPENDS_ON, RELATES_TO, CAUSED_BY, etc.
    strength REAL DEFAULT 1.0,          -- 0-1 edge weight
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    metadata TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (from_id) REFERENCES memory_nodes(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (to_id) REFERENCES memory_nodes(id),
    UNIQUE(from_id, to_id, relationship)
);

-- ==============================================================
-- CONVERSATION_TURNS: Three-tier history system
-- ==============================================================
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS conversation_turns (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    role TEXT NOT NULL,                 -- user, assistant, system
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    tokens INTEGER,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    metadata TEXT,
    tier TEXT DEFAULT 'active',         -- 'active', 'recent', 'archive'
    compressed TEXT,                    -- Compressed summary (for recent tier)
    compressed_at REAL,
    archived_at REAL,
    context_refs TEXT                   -- JSON array of referenced context IDs
);

-- ==============================================================
-- MEMORY_EMBEDDINGS: sqlite-vec virtual table for ANN search
-- Created dynamically when sqlite-vec extension is loaded
-- ==============================================================
-- CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_embeddings USING vec0(
--   id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
--   embedding FLOAT[1536]              -- OpenAI text-embedding-3-small dimension
-- );

-- ==============================================================
-- INDEXES
-- ==============================================================
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_type ON memory_nodes(node_type);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_created ON memory_nodes(created_at);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_importance ON memory_nodes(importance DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_accessed ON memory_nodes(last_accessed DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_lock_in ON memory_nodes(lock_in DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_nodes_lock_in_state ON memory_nodes(lock_in_state);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_from ON graph_edges(from_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_to ON graph_edges(to_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_edges_relationship ON graph_edges(relationship);

-- ==============================================================
-- TRIGGERS: Keep everything in sync
-- ==============================================================

-- Update timestamp on node modification
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_node_timestamp
AFTER UPDATE ON memory_nodes
BEGIN
    UPDATE memory_nodes SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id;
END;

-- Track access patterns
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS track_node_access
AFTER UPDATE OF access_count ON memory_nodes
BEGIN
    UPDATE memory_nodes SET last_accessed = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id;
END;

rows: "We chose Actor model over Hub Daemon"

subsections.title: Search Architecture
subsections.content: Luna uses multiple search strategies that can be combined based on query characteristics.

diagram.title: Current Implementation
diagram.description: QUERY branches to three parallel paths: FTS5 (Full-text, BM25 rank, ~10ms), sqlite-vec (Semantic vectors, ~10ms), and GRAPH (Traversal BFS, ~5ms). All three merge into RESULT MERGING (RRF).

subsections.implementation_note: **Implementation Note:** FTS5 is fully implemented with Porter stemmer and unicode61 tokenizer. The `fts5_search()` method uses BM25 ranking. A `search_nodes()` method also exists using LIKE patterns as a fallback. The `hybrid_search()` method combines FTS5 + semantic search using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF).

subsections.title: The sqlite-vec Advantage: Filtered Search
subsections.content: With sqlite-vec, filtering happens in the query itself:

code.content:
-- Filter and search together
SELECT n.*, vec_distance_cosine(e.embedding, ?) as distance
FROM memory_embeddings e
JOIN memory_nodes n ON e.id = n.id
WHERE n.node_type = 'FACT'
  AND n.created_at > '2025-01-01'
ORDER BY distance
LIMIT 10;

subsections.result: **Result:** Faster filtered queries, fewer wasted comparisons.

subsections.title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)
subsections.content: When combining multiple search results, RRF merges by rank position (not raw scores):

rows: Docs in multiple lists rank higher

subsections.title: Unified Query API
subsections.content: The Matrix provides a single interface that combines all search paths:

code.content:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional
from datetime import datetime

@dataclass
class MemoryNode:
    id: str
    node_type: str           # FACT, DECISION, PROBLEM, ACTION, CONTEXT
    content: str
    summary: Optional[str]
    source: Optional[str]
    confidence: float = 1.0
    importance: float = 0.5
    access_count: int = 0
    reinforcement_count: int = 0
    lock_in: float = 0.15
    lock_in_state: str = "drifting"
    metadata: dict = {}

class MemoryMatrix:
    """
    Unified memory store with hybrid search.
    Single file, all indexes unified.
    """

    async def search_nodes(
        self,
        query: str,
        node_types: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        limit: int = 10,
        use_embeddings: bool = True
    ) -> list[MemoryNode]:
        """
        Search memory nodes by content.

        - Extracts keywords from query
        - Searches compound phrases first
        - Falls back to individual terms
        - Optionally uses vector similarity
        """
        ...

    async def get_context(
        self,
        query: str,
        max_tokens: int = 2000
    ) -> list[MemoryNode]:
        """
        Get relevant context for a query.

        - Detects backward references ("what did we...")
        - Filters confusing/irrelevant nodes
        - Respects token budget
        """
        ...

subsections.title: Budget Presets
subsections.content: When fetching context for the Director:

subsections.title: Query Routing
subsections.content: Not every query needs all search paths. Route based on query characteristics.

code.content:
class QueryRouter:
    def route(self, query: str) -> SearchPath:
        """Determine optimal search path."""

        entities = extract_entities(query)
        keyword_density = self._keyword_density(query)
        is_conceptual = self._is_conceptual(query)

        # Multiple entities - relationship query
        if entities and len(entities) >= 2:
            return SearchPath.GRAPH

        # High keyword density - pattern match
        if keyword_density > 0.6:
            return SearchPath.KEYWORD

        # Abstract/conceptual - semantic search
        if is_conceptual:
            return SearchPath.VECTOR

        # Default to hybrid
        return SearchPath.HYBRID
